arts.ttl: Three questions and three answers from Getafe 0-1 Real Madrid
arts.sum: Real Madrid avoided any contact injuries, but did lose two players, Eduardo Camavinga and David Alaba, to muscular injuries with Carlo Ancelotti claiming that it could be difficult for either player to be involved in the Copa del Rey final on Saturday. The goal came from a long-range effort from Arda Güler in the first-half, but it was then backs against the wall for Real Madrid at times as José Bordalás’ team threatened until the death.

arts.ttl: Liverpool news: Trent Alexander-Arnold handed warning as Reds star makes Real Madrid admission
arts.sum: Arne Slot and Liverpool didn't get the present of early title celebrations, but Arsenal's draw with Crystal Palace leaves the Reds needing one point to clinch the title

arts.ttl: Bordalas: “With injuries like Carvajal’s, Madrid has had many defensive problems.”
arts.sum: The manager acknowledged recent struggles, citing inconsistency and discomfort with tactical shifts as the team tried to adapt. We stood up to a team like Real Madrid.

arts.ttl: Arsenal 'convinced' Mikel Arteta transfer target will join in summer ahead of Real Madrid
arts.sum: Arsenal are reportedly confident that one of their main summer transfer targets will join the club instead of Real Madrid to play for Mikel Arteta.

arts.ttl: Courtois: "Coming away from Getafe with three points gives us confidence for what lies ahead"
arts.sum: Thibaut Courtois played a key role in the team's victory over Getafe and he spoke to the press following the final whistle in the matchday 33 fixture: "We were a bit sluggish early on and we were struggling to move the ball, but we tried to up the pace a bit and we made it 1-0. Then they started to press a bit higher and we found it hard to pick the right balls and find people in space.

arts.ttl: Ancelotti: "We won the game with a very strong first half, and struggled more in the second"
arts.sum: “He played inside and he did it in his own way, a very good performance showing great quality and a sense of calm. Carlo Ancelotti spoke to the media gathered at the Coliseum press room to discuss Real Madrid's win over Getafe on LaLiga matchday 33: “The goal was to take three points and we did that, with a very strong first half, and then we struggled a bit in the second because Getafe pressed us more and we found it hard to keep control of the ball."

arts.ttl: Transfer rumors, news: Real Madrid, Liverpool, eye Kerkez
arts.sum: - Real Madrid, Liverpool and Manchester City are all interested in AFC Bournemouth left-back Milos Kerkez, as reported by Sky Sports . - Juventus have made Victor Osimhen their priority for the summer transfer window, reports Calciomercato, and sporting director Cristiano Giuntoli will soon start work on a deal for the Napoli striker currently on loan at Galatasaray.

arts.ttl: Managing Madrid Podcast: Getafe 0 - 1 Real Madrid, Post-game, La Liga 2024 - 2025
arts.sum: On this episode of the Managing Madrid Podcast, Kiyan Sobhani and Leah Revelle discuss: Kiyan and Leah break down Güler’s performance, 2nd half meltdown, Camavinga’s injury, and more.

arts.ttl: Ancelotti: “Real Madrid lack a bit of effectiveness up top”
arts.sum: Ancelotti on whether he was upset with the second half Real Madrid coach Carlo Ancelotti spoke after the 1-0 win over Getafe, reflecting on the performance by saying, “we have put emphasis on being more solid at the back...

arts.ttl: Real Madrid quoted affordable price to appoint Xabi Alonso as Carlo Ancelotti replacement
arts.sum: In recent weeks, there has been lots of speculation about the manager’s job at Real Madrid, which could soon be vacant. Carlo Ancelotti is currently in charge, but should he end the season on a downer, it is almost certain that his second spell as head coach would be brought to a premature end.
